data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T204719-1196cff0/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T204719-1196cff0/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T204719-1196cff0/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T204719-1196cff0/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T204719-1196cff0/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-451cdead">
    <title>docs(specs): codify cheapest-first/escalate/auto-recover principles across ADR-024, TD-031, SD-025, FEAT-010</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  The escalation+auto-recovery design (sister beads 20-25 implement)
  introduces new behavior that requires updates to FOUR governing
  spec documents, not just ADR-024:

  - ADR-024: power escalation + reviewer routing — needs explicit
    P1-P5 principles and within-cycle vs cross-cycle sequencing.
  - TD-031 §5: outcome → label/event/Extra mapping — needs new rows
    for reframe_applied, decompose_applied, auto_recovery_failed,
    per_bead_budget_exhausted.
  - TD-031 §6: claim semantics + role catalogue — needs reframer +
    decomposer roles defined with their dispatch contracts.
  - TD-031: state tracking — needs a new Extra field
    consecutive_ladder_exhaustions to drive the auto-recovery
    trigger.
  - SD-025: 3-layer execution lifecycle — auto-recovery is a NEW
    stage (between Layer 3 and status=proposed); SD-025 is silent.
  - FEAT-010: task execution feature spec — auto-recovery is a
    functional requirement, parallel to the "network-free drain
    boundary" added by sister bead ddx-3d5197d8.

  Without coherent spec coverage, implementers (sister beads 20-25)
  have nothing to anchor against; later operators can't tell
  whether observed behaviors are intentional. This bead is doc-only;
  it lands the full spec frame so the implementation beads can
  reference and conform.

CURRENT STATE
  - docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md
    governs power escalation but has no explicit principles
    enumeration, no within/cross-cycle sequencing rule.
  - docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md
    §5 has 14 outcome rows; missing reframe_applied,
    decompose_applied, auto_recovery_failed,
    per_bead_budget_exhausted, push_failed, push_conflict (last two
    covered by sister bead at rank 14).
  - TD-031 §6 (Claim Semantics) does not list reframer or
    decomposer as legitimate role types.
  - docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md
    Layer 3 (queue drain) does not mention auto-recovery; the
    only escape from drain is described as status=proposed.
  - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md
    Requirements section enumerates execution requirements but no
    auto-recovery requirement.
  - User memory feedback_models.md captures the cost-tiered
    intent but is not in spec form.

PROPOSED FIX

  1. ADR-024 amendments (primary anchor):
     - Add Principles section enumerating P1-P5 (cheapest first,
       escalate on failure, reviewer always stronger, reframe +
       decompose on persistent failure, no human-in-the-loop).
     - Add Sequencing section with explicit ordering:
       within-cycle (ladder climb) → cross-cycle (reframe) →
       cross-cycle (decompose) → status=proposed (final escape).
     - Add Per-bead budget subsection: a bead's escalation must
       stop when its per-bead cost exceeds the configured limit.
     - Add Operator escape hatches subsection: labels
       recovery:manual (skip auto-recovery), budget:<USD>
       (override default per-bead cost cap).

  2. TD-031 §5 amendments (outcome → mapping table):
     - New row: reframe_applied — bead description/AC rewritten by
       reframer agent; status stays open; events: reframe-applied
       with from-rev, to-rev, reframer-cost.
     - New row: decompose_applied — children filed via Store.Create
       with parent linked; parent status=open with
       execution-eligible=false; events: decompose-applied with
       child-IDs and reframer-cost.
     - New row: auto_recovery_failed — both reframe and decompose
       attempts failed; status=proposed; events:
       auto-recovery-failed with both attempt-cost values.
     - New row: per_bead_budget_exhausted — bead unclaimed without
       cooldown; events: per-bead-budget-exhausted with total cost.

  3. TD-031 §6 amendments (Claim semantics + role catalogue):
     - Add reframer role: dispatched with strong-tier MinPower (per
       ADR-024 P3); claim is held by drain proxy not the agent;
       output: structured edits to bead description/AC.
     - Add decomposer role: same dispatch rules; output: list of
       2-5 child bead specs to Store.Create.
     - Add new Extra field: consecutive_ladder_exhaustions —
       integer counter incremented when ladder exhausts on a
       drain cycle; cleared on success or operator action; drives
       auto-recovery trigger threshold (default 2).

  4. SD-025 amendments (3-layer execution lifecycle):
     - Add a new section "Layer 3.5: auto-recovery" between Layer
       3 (queue drain) and the proposed status escape:
       - Triggered after consecutive_ladder_exhaustions >= 2.
       - Routes to reframer first; on reframer failure or no
         change, routes to decomposer; on decomposer failure,
         falls through to status=proposed.
     - Cross-reference TD-031 §5 new rows.
     - Cross-reference ADR-024 P4.

  5. FEAT-010 amendments (Requirements section):
     - Add functional requirement: "Automatic recovery from
       persistent failure: when a bead's ladder is exhausted on
       N consecutive cycles, the system attempts (a) reframe via
       a strong-tier reframer agent, then (b) decomposition via
       a strong-tier decomposer agent, before parking to
       status=proposed."
     - Add NFR: "Per-bead cost budget: every bead's escalation
       attempts are bounded by a per-bead cost cap; budget
       exhaustion is a recheckable signal, not a final state."

  6. Cross-references throughout: each new section/row should
     point to the relevant sister bead and to the other affected
     specs.

NON-SCOPE
  - Implementation (each sister bead handles one concern).
  - The actual reframer/decomposer prompt templates (sister bead
    25 owns those).
  - Operator-facing tooling for manual reframe/decompose (CLI
    commands; not in scope of this spec amendment).

INTERSECTIONS
  - All sister beads in this batch (rank 20-25) implement against
    these specs.
  - Sister bead at rank 14 (TD-031 push_failed/push_conflict
    rows) — same TD-031 file, coordinate the two amendments.
  - Sister bead ddx-3d5197d8 (FEAT-010 network-free Land) — same
    FEAT-010 file, same Requirements section.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md contains a Principles section (P1-P5), a Sequencing section (within-cycle → cross-cycle → proposed), a Per-bead budget subsection, and an Operator escape hatches subsection (labels recovery:manual and budget:<USD>).
2. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md §5 contains 4 new outcome rows: reframe_applied, decompose_applied, auto_recovery_failed, per_bead_budget_exhausted.
3. TD-031 §6 lists reframer and decomposer role types with their dispatch contracts.
4. TD-031 documents a new Extra field consecutive_ladder_exhaustions with semantics (counter, clears on success/operator-action, drives auto-recovery trigger threshold).
5. docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md contains a Layer 3.5 (auto-recovery) section between Layer 3 and the status=proposed escape, with cross-references to TD-031 §5 and ADR-024 P4.
6. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md Requirements section contains a functional requirement on automatic recovery and an NFR on per-bead cost budget.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:docs, area:agent, kind:doc, spec:ADR-024, prevention</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="ADR-024" base-rev="2385d65a67cec0d36880a4f5053f87461abe6435" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T204719-1196cff0"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="ADR-024" path="docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md">ADR-024: Power Escalation and Review Routing Policy</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

ddx-451cdead # 1     +4s tool `{command="git add docs/helix/02-design/...", timeout=120000}` done 172ms

data.output:
All 4 files changed, hooks green, commit made. Here's what each AC maps to:

1. **ADR-024 Principles + Sequencing + Per-bead budget + Operator escape hatches** — added as top-level `## Principles` section (P1–P5), `### Escalation Sequencing`, `### Per-Bead Budget`, and `### Operator Escape Hatches` subsections.
2. **TD-031 §5 new rows** — `reframe_applied`, `decompose_applied`, `auto_recovery_failed`, `per_bead_budget_exhausted` appended to the outcome table.
3. **TD-031 §6 role catalogue** — `Auto-Recovery Role Catalogue` subsection added listing reframer and decomposer roles with dispatch contracts.
4. **TD-031 `consecutive_ladder_exhaustions`** — documented in the new §6 subsection with counter semantics, clear-on-success rule, and threshold config.
5. **SD-025 Layer 3.5** — new section between Layer 3 and Stop Conditions, with cross-refs to TD-031 §5 and ADR-024 P4.
6. **FEAT-010 FR #17 + per-bead cost budget NFR** — added to the Requirements section.
7. **`lefthook run pre-commit`** — passed clean.
